response: Data collection begins with identifying the sources of data needed for the study and ensuring data accuracy throughout the process. Data entry is crucial for maintaining data integrity and organizing data systematically. Data analysis involves interpreting data patterns and using data to draw meaningful conclusions. Data validation ensures that data quality remains high and data consistency is preserved. Data storage requires secure methods to protect data and ensure data accessibility when required. Data interpretation relies on examining data relationships and using data to support hypotheses. Data presentation involves organizing data clearly and making data understandable to stakeholders. Data evaluation concludes the study by assessing data outcomes and refining data practices for future research.
